page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 180 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core, fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the moderate 2.2 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core and parallelized execution are highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks scoring in the top 25%. Most notably, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, alongside strong text generation speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-96 offers high resource density by bundling eight enterprise-grade GPUs with a high-vCPU host, making it highly efficient for parallelized workloads despite the single-core performance trade-offs. It is optimized for large-scale LLM inference, machine learning workloads, high-throughput Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server equipped with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical Intel Xeon cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM. It features eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs with a total of 180 GB VRAM, while offering no local storage. Performance benchmarks highlight a significant contrast: single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, but multi-core throughput and memory bandwidth are strong. The server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. This hardware profile makes it highly suitable for GPU-accelerated machine learning, parallelized data processing, and high-throughput Redis operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384GB RAM, and 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs. It delivers top-tier LLM inference and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 384GB RAM, and 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
tagline: A high-density GPU platform combining eight accelerators with ninety-six vCPUs for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 180 GB total VRAM for accelerated computing
96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Strong multi-core CPU throughput and high-speed memory bandwidth
Optimized for machine learning, parallel data processing, and Redis caching
